---
title: "Viewership Analysis of Game of Thrones"
author: "Kuo Zhang"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| include: false

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)

file_paths <- list.files(
  "data",
  pattern = "^season_[0-9]+\\.csv$",
  full.names = TRUE
)

stopifnot(length(file_paths) == 8)

got_all <- lapply(file_paths, function(path) {
  season_num <- as.integer(sub("season_([0-9]+)\\.csv", "\\1", basename(path)))
  df <- read.csv(path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  df$season <- season_num
  df
}) |>
  bind_rows() |>
  mutate(
    viewers = as.numeric(viewers),
    no_season = as.integer(no_season)
  ) |>
  arrange(season, no_season)

season_summary <- got_all |>
  group_by(season) |>
  summarise(
    episodes = n(),
    avg_viewers = round(mean(viewers, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    max_viewers = round(max(viewers, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    min_viewers = round(min(viewers, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    change_avg_viewers = round(avg_viewers - lag(avg_viewers), 2)
  )
```

# Introduction

*Game of Thrones* is an American fantasy drama television series created by David Benioff and D. B. Weiss for HBO. It is based on *A Song of Ice and Fire* by George R. R. Martin. This report presents a simple analysis of the viewership of the show across eight seasons.

# Show Image

![Game of Thrones logo](images/got_logo.jpg){fig-align="center" width="60%"}

# Basic Statistics

```{r}
knitr::kable(
  season_summary,
  caption = "Summary statistics by season."
)
```

# Viewership Over Time

```{r}
ggplot(season_summary, aes(x = season, y = avg_viewers)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:8) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Viewership Across Seasons",
    x = "Season",
    y = "Average viewers (millions)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

# Season-to-Season Change in Viewership

```{r}
ggplot(season_summary[-1, ], aes(x = season, y = change_avg_viewers)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = change_avg_viewers), vjust = -0.3, size = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2:8) +
  labs(
    title = "Change in Average Viewership from Previous Season",
    x = "Season",
    y = "Change in average viewers (millions)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

# Short Interpretation

Average viewership increased from Season 1 (`r season_summary$avg_viewers[season_summary$season == 1]` million) 
to Season 8 (`r season_summary$avg_viewers[season_summary$season == 8]` million).  

The highest average viewership was observed in Season 
`r season_summary$season[which.max(season_summary$avg_viewers)]`, 
with `r max(season_summary$avg_viewers)` million viewers.  

The lowest average viewership was observed in Season 
`r season_summary$season[which.min(season_summary$avg_viewers)]`, 
with `r min(season_summary$avg_viewers)` million viewers.  

The largest increase from the previous season occurred in Season 
`r season_summary$season[which.max(season_summary$change_avg_viewers)]`, 
when average viewership rose by 
`r max(season_summary$change_avg_viewers, na.rm = TRUE)` million viewers.  

Overall, the show became more popular over time, although growth between Season 4 and Season 5 was almost flat.
